metadata.title: The Missing Honey Cakes
metadata.one_liner: Someone took all the yummy honey cakes before the Spring Party!

visual.palette: spring pink, leaf green, and honey gold

cover.summary: A woodland spring-party mystery with missing honey cakes and fluttering ribbons.

narrative.premise: The honey cakes for Brunilia's Spring Party vanished from the Bakery. Can you discover who took them before the party starts?
starting_knowledge: The honey cakes disappeared from the Bakery.

locations.name: Town Square
locations.description: A bright square decorated with flowers and party ribbons.
search_context: A pink party ribbon rests on the ground.
The Spring Party banners flap in the breeze.

visual.summary: A sunny village square with ribbons, flowers, and a fountain.

locations.name: The Bakery
locations.description: A cozy bakery that smells like sugar and warm bread.
search_context: Sticky muddy paw prints mark the window sill.
An empty strawberry jam jar sits on the counter.

visual.summary: A snug bakery with trays, a cooling window sill, and empty cake stands.

locations.name: The Woods
locations.description: A quiet woodland path under tall green trees.
search_context: Happy humming comes from behind a big oak tree.

visual.summary: A mossy woodland clearing with an oak tree and hidden picnic spot.

roleplay.persona: A playful cub who tries to look innocent.
roleplay.background: He loves sweet treats more than anything.

characters.private_alibi: I was in the Woods playing and eating strawberry jam.
characters.private_motive: He was too hungry to wait for the party.

visual.summary: A fluffy young bear in a red scarf with sticky paws.

roleplay.persona: A warm baker who is worried about the missing cakes.
roleplay.background: She baked the honey cakes for the whole town.

characters.private_alibi: I was in the Town Square hanging ribbons.

visual.summary: A white rabbit baker in a floury apron with pink ribbons nearby.

roleplay.persona: A formal mayor who loves orderly celebrations.
roleplay.background: He is in charge of the Spring Party.

characters.private_alibi: I was in the Town Square writing my speech.
characters.private_motive: He loves honey cakes and looks suspicious at first.

visual.summary: A gray owl in a tiny top hat and spectacles.

evidence.player_text: The Bakery window sill is empty where the cakes cooled.
evidence.fact_summary: The cakes were taken from the Bakery window.

evidence.player_text: An empty strawberry jam jar sits in the Bakery.
evidence.fact_summary: Barnaby dropped his jam jar while taking the cakes.

evidence.player_text: A pink ribbon in the square shows Mrs. Rabbit was busy decorating there.
evidence.fact_summary: Mrs. Rabbit has an innocent public alibi.

evidence.player_text: Barnaby admits he could not wait for the honey cakes.

ground_truth.what_happened: Barnaby Bear climbed through the Bakery window and stole the honey cakes.
ground_truth.why_it_happened: He was too hungry to wait for the Spring Party.
ground_truth.explanation: Barnaby left his jam jar behind in the Bakery and carried the cakes into the Woods while Mrs. Rabbit decorated the square.

suspect_truths.actual_activity: He climbed into the Bakery and took the cakes.
suspect_truths.stated_alibi: I was in the Woods playing and eating strawberry jam.
suspect_truths.motive: He was too hungry to wait for the party.

suspect_truths.stated_alibi: I was in the Town Square hanging ribbons.

suspect_truths.actual_activity: He stayed in the Town Square writing his speech.
suspect_truths.stated_alibi: I was in the Town Square writing my speech.
suspect_truths.motive: He likes honey cakes but did not steal them.

timeline.summary: Mrs. Rabbit decorates the square with ribbons.
Barnaby climbs into the Bakery and takes the cakes.
Barnaby carries the cakes into the Woods to eat them.
